mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly lower when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive Rank IC > 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production during an economic expansion reflects robust manufacturing output that supports near-term corporate revenue growth and equity prices.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.02) when applied outside expansion regimes, or 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 300.0 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the threshold shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, producing positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or payroll growth fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth above 2.0% and unemployment rates below 4.0% define a strong demand regime where corporate earnings expand rapidly, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction effect yields a negative or insignificant Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly positive when both conditions hold.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and retail sales during a recessionary phase signals collapsing economic activity that forces earnings downgrades and triggers negative equity repricing over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances during recessions, or the joint decline shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.
